metadata.description: Hand-curated past paper questions mapped to AQA spec codes and James's RAG topics. Each question tagged with the primary spec references it exercises — many questions are synoptic and span multiple codes. Use this to generate topic-filtered practice lists.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: With the help of a diagram, analyse how a trade union might achieve higher pay for its members.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Assess the view that government intervention in the UK labour market is necessary to protect the interests of people who are working in the gig economy.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: With the help of a diagram, analyse the impact on grocery consumers of interdependence between supermarkets.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate the view that the supermarket sector is serving customers' interests well.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Assess the view that high-speed Internet connection is a necessity for modern life and should be provided by the government, free of charge, to all households.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate the view that government failure means that government intervention in markets will rarely lead to an improvement in economic welfare.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate whether the best way to reduce inequality in disposable income is to reduce differences in pre-tax incomes rather than through the tax and benefits system.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Explain the factors that a commercial aircraft manufacturer, such as Boeing or Airbus, should consider when forecasting the future sales of its aircraft.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Would you recommend that the UK government provides financial support for the companies involved in the production of commercial aircraft? Justify your recommendation.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: With the help of a diagram, explain why the production and sale of lithium-ion batteries might lead to market failure.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate policies that could be used to reduce the environmental impact of motor vehicle use.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: With the help of a diagram, explain how a reduction in out-of-work benefits may lead to lower wages in some labour markets.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Using the extracts and your knowledge of economics, evaluate policies that could be used to reduce in-work poverty in the UK.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Explain why, in long-run equilibrium, monopolistically competitive markets are neither productively nor allocatively efficient.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Assess the view that competition policy is likely to lead to markets becoming less efficient in the long run.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate the view that labour markets work best when strong monopsony power is balanced by trade union power.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Discuss the view that privatisation is always beneficial because it leads to improvements in efficiency.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: With the help of a diagram, explain how high economic growth could help to create a budget surplus.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate the view that a rise in Ireland's corporation tax rate is likely to have a damaging effect on its macroeconomic performance.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: With the help of a diagram, explain how reduced availability of credit for households and firms damaged the real economy and created high unemployment.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Using the data in the extracts and your knowledge of economics, assess the view that excessive use of credit is always damaging to an economy.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Assess the view that increased globalisation has been beneficial for the UK economy.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Explain the possible causes of a shift in the short-run aggregate supply curve to the left.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate how, in the long-run, governments could try to reconcile conflicts between the various objectives of economic policy.

questions.subject: econ
questions.text: Evaluate the view that economic growth usually leads to an improvement in living standards.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Explain and analyse three ways in which the media can have an influence upon the outcome of elections.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Explain and analyse three ways in which the Official Opposition can challenge the government in the House of Commons.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Explain and analyse three ways in which UK political parties select their leaders.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Analyse, evaluate and compare the arguments in the above extracts regarding the need for a codified constitution in the UK.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: 'The UK judiciary has had an increasingly significant impact upon the working of the executive and Parliament since 1997.' Analyse and evaluate.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Nationalism: 'Nationalism is based on a clear view of a country's culture and traditions.' Analyse and evaluate this statement.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Analyse, evaluate and compare the arguments made in the above extracts on the role of the state.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Explain and analyse three ways in which the results of elections have an impact on the types of government formed in the UK.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Explain and analyse three methods used by UK pressure groups to achieve their aims.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Analyse, evaluate and compare the arguments in the above extracts regarding the roles and influence of parliamentary select committees.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: 'Cabinet ministers still matter as heads of department, but the whole cabinet is no longer at the centre of important government decision-making.' Analyse and evaluate.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: 'The UK is experiencing a crisis in political participation.' Analyse and evaluate this statement.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Socialism: Explain and analyse three ways in which socialist thinkers view the concept of class.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Analyse, evaluate and compare the arguments made in the above extracts about freedom and its limitations.

questions.subject: pol
questions.text: Nationalism: 'Nationalism is incompatible with the concept of equality between all people.' Analyse and evaluate this statement.
